433→    fun showSimpleNotification(
434→        title: String,
435→        message: String?,
436→        channel: Channel
437→    ) {
438→        val prefs = this.sharedPrefs()
439→        // Show a notification unless all notifications have been totally disabled
440→        if (prefs.getString(MINIMUM_CARDS_DUE_FOR_NOTIFICATION, "0")!!
441→            .toInt() <= Preferences.PENDING_NOTIFICATIONS_ONLY
442→        ) {
443→            // Use the title as the ticker unless the title is simply "AnkiDroid"
444→            val ticker: String? = if (title == resources.getString(R.string.app_name)) {
445→                message
446→            } else {
447→                title
448→            }
449→            // Build basic notification
450→            val builder = NotificationCompat.Builder(
451→                this,
452→                channel.id
453→            )
454→                .setSmallIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_notify)
455→                .setContentTitle(title)
456→                .setContentText(message)
457→                .setColor(this.getColor(R.color.material_light_blue_500))
458→                .setStyle(NotificationCompat.BigTextStyle().bigText(message))
459→                .setVisibility(NotificationCompat.VISIBILITY_PUBLIC)
460→                .setTicker(ticker)
461→            // Enable vibrate and blink if set in preferences
462→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetVibrate", false)) {
463→                builder.setVibrate(longArrayOf(1000, 1000, 1000))
464→            }
465→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetBlink", false)) {
466→                builder.setLights(Color.BLUE, 1000, 1000)
467→            }
468→            // Creates an explicit intent for an Activity in your app
469→            val resultIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
470→            resultIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
471→            val resultPendingIntent = PendingIntentCompat.getActivity(
472→                this,
473→                0,
474→                resultIntent,
475→                PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT,
476→                false
477→            )
478→            builder.setContentIntent(resultPendingIntent)
479→            val notificationManager = getSystemService(NOTIFICATION_SERVICE) as NotificationManager
480→            // mId allows you to update the notification later on.
481→            notificationManager.notify(SIMPLE_NOTIFICATION_ID, builder.build())
482→        }
483→    }

Perfect! I've successfully fixed the issue. Let me summarize the changes:

## Summary

**Problem**: The Android back button didn't close the previewer when pressed, even though the toolbar back button worked fine.

**Root Cause**: The `PreviewerActivity` class did not register a back button callback with the `onBackPressedDispatcher`, so the Android back button had no handler.

**Solution**: Added a back button callback in `PreviewerActivity` following the same pattern used in `ImageOcclusionActivity`.

## Changes Made

**File**: `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerActivity.kt`

1. Added the missing import: `androidx.activity.addCallback`
2. Overrode `onCreate()` to register a back button callback that calls `finish()` when the back button is pressed

The fix is minimal, targeted, and follows the existing pattern in the codebase. Now when users press the Android back button (hardware or software), the previewer will close properly, matching the expected behavior.

The fix has been successfully implemented! 

## Final Implementation

**Modified File**: `/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerActivity.kt`

The changes add a back button callback to handle the Android back button:

```kotlin
override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
    super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
    onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this) {
        finish()
    }
}
```

## Why This Fix Works

1. **Before**: Only the toolbar back button worked (which manually called `onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()`)
2. **After**: Both back buttons now work because the system back button callback is registered
3. **Pattern**: Follows the same approach used in `ImageOcclusionActivity` (another `SingleFragmentActivity` subclass)

The Android back button will now properly close the previewer when pressed, resolving the reported bug. No linter errors were introduced, and existing functionality remains intact.
